paragraph.header: East fork san gabriel river, california
paragraph.text: The 12.7-mile segment from the confluence of the Prairie Fork and Vincent Gulch to 100 yards upstream of the confluence with Williams Canyon.

paragraph.header: North fork san gabriel river, california
paragraph.text: The 4.3-mile segment from the confluence with Cloudburst Canyon to 0.25 miles upstream of the confluence with the West Fork San Gabriel River.

paragraph.header: West fork san gabriel river, california
paragraph.text: The 8.3-mile segment from 0.25 miles downstream of its source near Red Box Gap in sec. 14, T. 2 N., R. 12 W., to the confluence with Bobcat Canyon.

subsection.text: Section 5(b) of the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act (16 U.S.C. 1276(b)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.header: East fork san gabriel river, california; north fork san gabriel river, california; west fork san gabriel river, california

subparagraph.text: Not later than 3 years after the date on which funds are made available to carry out this paragraph, the Secretary of Agriculture shall—

clause.text: complete each of the studies described in paragraphs (147) through (149) of subsection (a); and
submit to Congress a report describing the results of each of those studies.

subparagraph.text: In carrying out each of the studies described in paragraphs (147) through (149) of subsection (a), the Secretary of Agriculture shall identify opportunities to administer the applicable segments described in those paragraphs in partnership with State, regional, local, and community stakeholders.

subsection.text: Subject to valid existing rights, there are established the following scenic areas:

paragraph.text: Certain land in the Los Padres National Forest comprising approximately 18,666 acres, as generally depicted on the map entitled Condor Ridge Scenic Area—Proposed and dated March 29, 2019, which shall be known as the Condor Ridge Scenic Area.

Certain land in the Los Padres National Forest and the Bakersfield Field Office of the Bureau of Land Management comprising approximately 16,216 acres, as generally depicted on the map entitled Black Mountain Scenic Area—Proposed and dated March 29, 2019, which shall be known as the Black Mountain Scenic Area.

As soon as practicable after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior shall file a map and legal description of the scenic areas established by subsection (a) (referred to in this section as the scenic areas) with—

subparagraph.text: the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate; and
the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives.

paragraph.text: The maps and legal descriptions filed under paragraph (1) shall have the same force and effect as if included in this Act, except that the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior may correct any clerical and typographical errors in the maps and legal descriptions.
The maps and legal descriptions filed under paragraph (1) shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the Forest Service and Bureau of Land Management.

subsection.text: The purpose of the scenic areas is to conserve, protect, and enhance for the benefit and enjoyment of present and future generations the ecological, scenic, wildlife, recreational, cultural, historical, natural, educational, and scientific resources of the scenic areas.

paragraph.text: The Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior shall administer land under their respective jurisdiction within the scenic areas—

subparagraph.text: in a manner that conserves, protects, and enhances the resources of the scenic areas, and in particular the scenic character attributes of the scenic areas; and
in accordance with—

clause.text: this section;
the Federal Land Policy and Management Act (43 U.S.C. 1701 et seq.) for land under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of the Interior;
any laws (including regulations) relating to the National Forest System, for land under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of Agriculture; and
any other applicable law (including regulations).

paragraph.text: The Secretary shall only allow those uses of the scenic areas that the Secretary determines would further the purposes described in subsection (c).

subsection.text: Subject to valid existing rights, the Federal land in the scenic areas is withdrawn from all forms of—

paragraph.text: entry, appropriation, or disposal under the public land laws;
location, entry, and patent under the mining laws; and
disposition under all laws pertaining to mineral and geothermal leasing or mineral materials.

subsection.text: The following shall be prohibited on the Federal land within the scenic areas:

paragraph.text: Permanent roads.
Permanent structures.
Timber harvesting, except when necessary for the purposes described in subsection (g).
Transmission lines.
Except as necessary to meet the minimum requirements for the administration of the scenic areas and to protect public health and safety—

subparagraph.text: the use of motorized vehicles; or
the establishment of temporary roads.

paragraph.text: Commercial enterprises, except as necessary for realizing the purposes of the scenic areas.

subsection.text: Consistent with this section, the Secretary may take any measures in the scenic areas that the Secretary determines to be necessary to control fire, insects, and diseases, including, as the Secretary determines to be appropriate, the coordination of those activities with the State or a local agency.
The fact that an otherwise authorized activity or use can be seen or heard within a scenic area shall not preclude the activity or use outside the boundary of the scenic area.

subparagraph.text: Subject to valid existing rights, there is established the Horse Mountain Special Management Area, comprising approximately 7,482 acres of Federal land in the Six Rivers National Forest, as generally depicted on the map entitled Horse Mountain Special Management Area and dated May 15, 2020.

The purpose of the Horse Mountain Special Management Area is to enhance the recreational and scenic values of the special management area while conserving the plants, wildlife, and other natural resource values of the area.

Subject to valid existing rights, there is established the Sanhedrin Special Management Area, comprising approximately 12,254 acres of Federal land in the Mendocino National Forest, as generally depicted on the map entitled Sanhedrin Special Management Area and dated November 14, 2023.
The purposes of the Sanhedrin Special Management Area are—

clause.text: to conserve, protect, and enhance for the benefit and enjoyment of present and future generations the ecological, scenic, wildlife, recreational, roadless, cultural, historical, natural, educational, and scientific resources of the area;
to protect and restore late-successional forest structure, oak woodlands and grasslands, aquatic habitat, and anadromous fisheries within the area;
to protect and restore the undeveloped character of the area; and
to allow visitors to enjoy the scenic, natural, cultural, and wildlife values of the area.

subparagraph.text: Subject to valid existing rights, there is established the Fox Mountain Special Management Area, comprising approximately 41,082 acres of Federal land in the Los Padres National Forest, as generally depicted on the map entitled Fox Mountain Special Management Area and dated November 14, 2023.
The purposes of the Fox Mountain Special Management Area are to conserve, protect, and enhance for the benefit and enjoyment of present and future generations—

clause.text: the ecological, scenic, wildlife, recreational, roadless, cultural, historical, natural, educational, and scientific resources of the area; and
the cultural and historical resources and values of the area.

paragraph.text: Not later than 5 years after the date of enactment of this Act and in accordance with paragraph (2), the Secretary of Agriculture (referred to in this section as the Secretary) shall develop a comprehensive plan for the long-term management of the special management areas established by subsection (a).
In developing the management plan required under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall consult with—

subparagraph.text: appropriate State, Tribal, and local governmental entities; and
members of the public.

paragraph.text: The management plan required under paragraph (1) shall ensure that recreational use within a special management area established by subsection (a) (referred to in this section as a special management area) does not cause significant adverse impacts on the plants and wildlife of the special management area.
The Secretary shall manage a special management area—

subparagraph.text: in furtherance of the purpose for the applicable special management area described in subsection (a); and
in accordance with—

clause.text: the laws (including regulations) generally applicable to the National Forest System;
this section; and
any other applicable law (including regulations).

paragraph.text: The Secretary shall only allow uses of a special management area that the Secretary determines would further the purposes of the applicable special management area described in subsection (a).

The Secretary shall continue to authorize, maintain, and enhance the recreational use of the special management areas, including hunting, fishing, camping, hiking, hang gliding, sightseeing, nature study, horseback riding, rafting, mountain bicycling, motorized recreation on authorized routes, and other recreational activities, if the recreational use is consistent with—

subparagraph.text: the purpose of the applicable special management area;
this section;
other applicable law (including regulations); and
any applicable management plans.

Except as provided in paragraph (C), the use of motorized vehicles in a special management area shall be permitted only on existing roads, trails, and areas designated for use by such vehicles as of the date of enactment of this Act.
Except as provided in paragraph (C), no new or temporary roads shall be constructed within a special management area.
Nothing in paragraph (A) or (B) prevents the Secretary from—

clause.text: rerouting or closing an existing road or trail to protect natural resources from degradation, or to protect public safety, as determined to be appropriate by the Secretary;
designating routes of travel on land acquired by the Secretary and incorporated into a special management area if the designations are—

subclause.text: consistent with the purposes of the applicable special management area described in subsection (a); and
completed, to the maximum extent practicable, not later than 3 years after the date of acquisition;

clause.text: constructing a temporary road on which motorized vehicles are permitted as part of a vegetation management project carried out in accordance with subparagraph (D);
authorizing the use of motorized vehicles for administrative purposes; or
responding to an emergency.
In this subparagraph, the term decommission means, with respect to a road—

subclause.text: to reestablish vegetation on the road; and
to restore any natural drainage, watershed function, or other ecological processes that are disrupted or adversely impacted by the road by removing or hydrologically disconnecting the road prism.

clause.text: Not later than 3 years after the date on which the applicable vegetation management project is completed, the Secretary shall decommission any temporary road constructed under subparagraph (C)(iii).

paragraph.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), no harvesting of timber shall be allowed within a special management area.
The Secretary may authorize harvesting of timber in a special management area established by subsection (a)—

subparagraph.text: if the Secretary determines that the harvesting is necessary to further the purposes of the special management area;
in a manner consistent with the purposes for the applicable special management area; and
subject to—

clause.text: such reasonable regulations, policies, and practices as the Secretary determines to be appropriate; and
all applicable laws (including regulations).

subsection.text: The grazing of livestock in a special management area, where established before the date of enactment of this Act, shall be permitted to continue—

paragraph.text: subject to—

subparagraph.text: such reasonable regulations, policies, and practices as the Secretary considers necessary; and
applicable law (including regulations); and

paragraph.text: in a manner consistent with the purposes of the applicable special management area described in subsection (a).

subsection.text: Consistent with this section, the Secretary may carry out any activities within a special management area that the Secretary determines to be necessary to control fire, insects, or diseases, including the coordination of those activities with a State or local agency.

subsection.header: Acquisition and incorporation of land and interests in land

paragraph.text: In accordance with applicable laws (including regulations), the Secretary may acquire any land or interest in land within or adjacent to the boundaries of a special management area by purchase from a willing seller, donation, or exchange.
Any land or interest in land acquired by the Secretary under paragraph (1) shall be—

subparagraph.text: incorporated into, and administered as part of, the applicable special management area; and
withdrawn in accordance with subsection (i).

subsection.text: To the maximum extent practicable and in accordance with applicable laws, on request of an affected federally recognized Indian Tribe, the Secretary of the Interior (acting through the Director of the Bureau of Land Management) and the Secretary of Agriculture (acting through the Chief of the Forest Service) shall enter into agreements, contracts, and other cooperative and collaborative partnerships with the federally recognized Indian Tribe regarding management of a special management area under relevant Federal authority, including—

paragraph.text: the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act (25 U.S.C. 5301 et seq.);
the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 (43 U.S.C. 1701 et seq.);
the Tribal Self-Governance Act of 1994 (25 U.S.C. 5361 et seq.);
the Tribal Forest Protection Act of 2004 (25 U.S.C. 3115a et seq.);
the good neighbor authority under section 8206 of the Agricultural Act of 2014 (16 U.S.C. 2113a);
Executive Order 13175 (25 U.S.C. 5301 note; relating to consultation and coordination with Indian Tribal governments);

Secretarial Order 3342, issued by the Secretary of the Interior on October 21, 2016 (relating to identifying opportunities for cooperative and collaborative partnerships with federally recognized Indian Tribes in the management of Federal lands and resources); and

Joint Secretarial Order 3403, issued by the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Agriculture on November 15, 2021 (relating to fulfilling the trust responsibility to Indian Tribes in the stewardship of Federal lands and waters).

subsection.text: Subject to valid existing rights, all Federal land located in a special management area is withdrawn from—

paragraph.text: all forms of entry, appropriation, and disposal under the public land laws;
location, entry, and patenting under the mining laws; and
operation of the mineral leasing, mineral materials, and geothermal leasing laws.

subsection.text: As soon as practicable after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall prepare maps and legal descriptions of—

paragraph.text: the South Fork Trinity-Mad River Restoration Area established by section 101(b);
the wilderness areas and wilderness additions designated, renamed, or modified by section 301 (or an amendment made by that section);
the potential wilderness areas designated by section 303(a); and
the Horse Mountain Special Management Area, Sanhedrin Special Management Area, and Fox Mountain Special Management Area established by section 306(a).

subsection.text: The maps and legal descriptions prepared under subsection (a) shall have the same force and effect as if included in this Act, except that the Secretary may correct any clerical and typographical errors in the maps and legal descriptions.

The maps and legal descriptions prepared under subsection (a) shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the Forest Service, the Bureau of Land Management, or the National Park Service, as applicable.

section.header: Updates to land and resource management plans
section.text: As soon as practicable after the date of enactment of this Act, in accordance with applicable law (including regulations), the Secretary shall incorporate the designations and studies required by this Act into updated management plans for units covered by this Act.

section.header: Pacific Gas and Electric Company utility facilities and rights-of-way

subsection.text: Nothing in this Act—

paragraph.text: affects any validly issued right-of-way for the customary operation, maintenance, upgrade, repair, relocation within an existing right-of-way, replacement, or other authorized activity (including the use of any mechanized vehicle, helicopter, and other aerial device) in a right-of-way acquired by or issued, granted, or permitted to Pacific Gas and Electric Company (including any predecessor or successor in interest or assign) that is located on land included in—

subparagraph.text: the South Fork Trinity-Mad River Restoration Area established by section 101(b);
the Bigfoot National Recreation Trail established under section 201(b)(1); or
the Horse Mountain Special Management Area or Sanhedrin Special Management Area established by section 306(a); or

paragraph.text: prohibits the upgrading or replacement of any—

subparagraph.text: utility facilities of the Pacific Gas and Electric Company, including those utility facilities in existence on the date of enactment of this Act within—

clause.text: the South Fork Trinity-Mad River Restoration Area known as—

subclause.text: Gas Transmission Line 177A or rights-of-way;
Gas Transmission Line DFM 1312–02 or rights-of-way;
Electric Transmission Line Bridgeville-Cottonwood 115 kV or rights-of-way;
Electric Transmission Line Humboldt-Trinity 60 kV or rights-of-way;
Electric Transmission Line Humboldt-Trinity 115 kV or rights-of-way;
Electric Transmission Line Maple Creek-Hoopa 60 kV or rights-of-way;
Electric Distribution Line-Willow Creek 1101 12 kV or rights-of-way;
Electric Distribution Line-Willow Creek 1103 12 kV or rights-of-way;
Electric Distribution Line-Low Gap 1101 12 kV or rights-of-way;
Electric Distribution Line-Fort Seward 1121 12 kV or rights-of-way;
Forest Glen Border District Regulator Station or rights-of-way;
Durret District Gas Regulator Station or rights-of-way;
Gas Distribution Line 4269C or rights-of-way;
Gas Distribution Line 43991 or rights-of-way;
Gas Distribution Line 4993D or rights-of-way;
Sportsmans Club District Gas Regulator Station or rights-of-way;
Highway 36 and Zenia District Gas Regulator Station or rights-of-way;
Dinsmore Lodge 2nd Stage Gas Regulator Station or rights-of-way;
Electric Distribution Line-Wildwood 1101 12 kV or rights-of-way;
Low Gap Substation;
Hyampom Switching Station; or
Wildwood Substation;

clause.text: the Bigfoot National Recreation Trail known as—

subclause.text: Gas Transmission Line 177A or rights-of-way;
Electric Transmission Line Humboldt-Trinity 115 kV or rights-of-way;
Electric Transmission Line Bridgeville-Cottonwood 115 kV or rights-of-way; or
Electric Transmission Line Humboldt-Trinity 60 kV or rights-of-way;

clause.text: the Sanhedrin Special Management Area known as Electric Distribution Line-Willits 1103 12 kV or rights-of-way; or
the Horse Mountain Special Management Area known as Electric Distribution Line Willow Creek 1101 12 kV or rights-of-way; or

subparagraph.text: utility facilities of the Pacific Gas and Electric Company in rights-of-way issued, granted, or permitted by the Secretary adjacent to a utility facility referred to in subparagraph (A).

subsection.text: Not later than the later of the date that is 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act or the date of issuance of a new utility facility right-of-way within the South Fork Trinity-Mad River Restoration Area, Bigfoot National Recreation Trail, Sanhedrin Special Management Area, or Horse Mountain Special Management Area, the Secretary, in consultation with the Pacific Gas and Electric Company, shall publish plans for regular and emergency access by the Pacific Gas and Electric Company to the inholdings and rights-of-way of the Pacific Gas and Electric Company.

section.header: Reauthorization of existing water facilities in Pleasant View Ridge Wilderness

subsection.text: The Secretary of Agriculture may issue a special use authorization to the owners of a water transport or diversion facility (referred to in this section as a facility) located on National Forest System land in the Pleasant View Ridge Wilderness designated by section 1802(8) of the Omnibus Public Land Management Act of 2009 (16 U.S.C. 1132 note; Public Law 111–11; 123 Stat. 1054) for the continued operation, maintenance, and reconstruction of the facility if the Secretary determines that—

paragraph.text: the facility was in existence on the date on which the land on which the facility is located was designated as part of the National Wilderness Preservation System (referred to in this section as the date of designation);
the facility has been in substantially continuous use to deliver water for the beneficial use on the non-Federal land of the owner since the date of designation;
the owner of the facility holds a valid water right for use of the water on the non-Federal land of the owner under State law, with a priority date that predates the date of designation; and

it is not practicable or feasible to relocate the facility to land outside of the Pleasant View Ridge Wilderness and continue the beneficial use of water on the non-Federal land recognized under State law.

subsection.text: A special use authorization issued under this section shall be subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary determines appropriate to protect wilderness resources and values.

section.header: Use by members of Indian Tribes

subsection.text: The Secretary shall ensure that Indian Tribes have access, in accordance with the Wilderness Act (16 U.S.C. 1131 et seq.), to the South Fork Trinity-Mad River Restoration Area, wilderness areas, scenic areas, special management areas, and potential wilderness areas designated by this Act for traditional cultural and religious purposes.

paragraph.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretary, on request of an Indian Tribe, may temporarily close to the general public 1 or more specific portions of a wilderness area, scenic area, or potential wilderness area designated by this Act to protect the privacy of the members of the Indian Tribe in the conduct of traditional cultural and religious activities.
Any closure under paragraph (1) shall be—

subparagraph.text: made in such a manner as to affect the smallest practicable area for the minimum period of time necessary for the activity to be carried out; and
be consistent with—

clause.text: Public Law 95–341 (commonly known as the American Indian Religious Freedom Act) (42 U.S.C. 1996 et seq.);
the Wilderness Act (16 U.S.C. 1131 et seq.); and
the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act (16 U.S.C. 1276 et seq.).
